name: Serradilla from Mirabel — circular through the Umbría de Barbechoso
name_original: Serradilla desde Mirabel — circular por la Umbría de Barbechoso
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mirabel traverse a landscape characterized by the Tajo River, rolling hills, and the natural beauty of Monfragüe National Park. The region features a mix of open rural roads and routes that pass through areas like the Umbría de Barbechoso and El Puerto de los Castaños. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from river valleys to moderate climbs, offering diverse experiences for road biking. Best road cycling routes around Mirabel The most popular road cycling route is Torrejón el Rubio from Mirabel — circular by the Tajo River — Monfragüe National Park, a 58.2 miles (93.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Tajo River and passes through Monfragüe National Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serradilla from Mirabel — circular through the Umbría de Barbechoso, a moderate 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path. It explores the Umbría de Barbechoso, featuring rolling hills and rural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Torrejoncillo from Mirabel — circular via El Puerto de los Castaños, a 43.3 miles (69.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including El Puerto de los Castaños, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Mirabel is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and rural roads. The network offers options for moderate to challenging ability levels. The routes in Mirabel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mirabel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mirabel?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;5&lt;/em&gt; road cycling routes around Mirabel featured in this guide, offering diverse experiences from moderate to difficult.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Mirabel?
A: Mirabel's climate, characterized by the Tajo River and rolling hills, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Mirabel?
A: Yes, Mirabel offers challenging routes for experienced road cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292266485/torrejon-el-rubio-from-mirabel-circular-by-the-tajo-river-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Mirabel — circular by the Tajo River — Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain over its 58.2 miles (93.6 km).

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Mirabel?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this selection, several moderate routes could be suitable for beginners looking for a bit of a challenge. The region's terrain includes rolling hills, so expect some climbs even on moderate paths.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Mirabel?
A: Absolutely. The routes often pass through areas with beautiful vistas, especially those near the Tajo River and within Monfragüe National Park. You can find stunning views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic scenery.

Q: Are there circular road bike routes available from Mirabel?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Mirabel are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292266485/torrejon-el-rubio-from-mirabel-circular-by-the-tajo-river-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Mirabel — circular by the Tajo River — Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284622405/serradilla-from-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-de-barbechoso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradilla from Mirabel — circular through the Umbría de Barbechoso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places can I explore near the road cycling routes in Mirabel?
A: The region around Mirabel is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. Cyclists can explore areas like the Umbría de Barbechoso and El Puerto de los Castaños. Notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the natural monument known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to see while cycling around Mirabel?
A: Yes, the area boasts several historical landmarks. You can encounter sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a glimpse into the region's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/655984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo City Walls&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galisteo Historic Town and Stone Archway&lt;/a&gt; are also significant historical points of interest.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Mirabel?
A: The road cycling routes in Mirabel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to rolling hills, and the beautiful natural landscapes, particularly those within Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Which routes offer views of the Tajo River?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292266485/torrejon-el-rubio-from-mirabel-circular-by-the-tajo-river-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Mirabel — circular by the Tajo River — Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt; route specifically offers extensive views of the Tajo River as it traverses the landscape.

Q: Are there routes that pass through Monfragüe National Park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292266485/torrejon-el-rubio-from-mirabel-circular-by-the-tajo-river-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from Mirabel — circular by the Tajo River — Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt; route is a prime example, taking you directly through this protected area known for its natural beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the road cycling routes around Mirabel?
A: The terrain around Mirabel is quite varied. You can expect a mix of open rural roads, rolling hills, and routes that pass through river valleys, such as those along the Tajo River. Areas like the Umbría de Barbechoso and El Puerto de los Castaños contribute to the diverse landscape.
tips: The ruins of Castillo de Mirabel are an imposing defensive fortress built on the western hills of the Monfragüe National Park. If you choose to go up to the castle, you will be treated to wonderful views of the Tagus Valley landscape below.
Beautiful road with curves that borders the waters of the reservoir bordered by the Dam of the Ribera del Castaño Reservoir.
One of the gateways to the Monfragüe National Park, Serradilla is a small town on the route between Plasencia and the historic city of Cáceres.
